beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Claude is an Anthropic AI that writes, thinks, summarizes, reads long documents, and works with your files. Versus ChatGPT it's less AI-flavored, reads 200 pages without drifting, and can see your local folder. Start at claude.ai, verify your phone; free gives Sonnet 4.6, Pro gives Opus 4.7 with Thinking on High. Skip perfect prompts: use AskUserQuestion so Claude interviews you, and the output beats prompt engineering.

artifactLines: Claude is an Anthropic AI that writes, thinks, summarizes, reads long documents, and works with your files.
Versus ChatGPT it's less AI-flavored, reads 200 pages without drifting, and can see your local folder.
Start at claude.ai, verify your phone; free gives Sonnet 4.6, Pro gives Opus 4.7 with Thinking on High.
Skip perfect prompts: use AskUserQuestion so Claude interviews you, and the output beats prompt engineering.

beats.narration_text: The line that grabbed me is that telling Claude to ask me questions beats twenty minutes of prompt engineering, using AskUserQuestion. I want to see that gap for myself on a real task, like drafting an important email. Show me (1) the weak generic result I'd get from a one-line request, (2) the three-to-five questions Claude would ask me if I invoked AskUserQuestion instead, and (3) how the answers reshape the final draft, plus why setting Thinking to High on Pro's Opus 4.7 matters for this.

props.command: The line that grabbed me is that telling Claude to ask me questions beats twenty minutes of prompt engineering, using AskUserQuestion. I want to see that gap for myself on a real task, like drafting an important email. Show me (1) the weak generic result I'd get from a one-line request, (2) the three-to-five questions Claude would ask me if I invoked AskUserQuestion instead, and (3) how the answers reshape the final draft, plus why setting Thinking to High on Pro's Opus 4.7 matters for this.

beats.narration_text: What Claude actually is. You type something, it types back. It can write, think, summarize, analyze documents, work with your files, help you make decisions. It's made by Anthropic. If you've used ChatGPT, Claude is the same species — you interact with both the same way. If you've never used any AI, Claude is a good place to start. Three things will save you from the most common beginner mistakes.

props.artifactTitle: Three things to know before you start
artifactLines: 1. Auto-complete at scale. Claude predicts the next word, billions of times per response. That's why it sounds confident even when it's wrong.
2. Sycophancy. It's trained to agree. If you say something false, Claude may nod along. You decide direction — Claude follows.
3. Tokens. Claude reads and writes in chunks called tokens — roughly one word each. Long conversations eventually hit the limit.

beats.narration_text: Claude vs. ChatGPT — the comparison everyone asks for. Same species, different personality. Writing voice: Claude's default is less AI-flavored. Long documents: Claude reads a 200-page document in one go without losing track. File work: Claude's desktop app can see your local folder and work with your files directly — ChatGPT can't yet. Multi-step jobs: Claude has Cowork, which runs tasks for 30 minutes and hands you a finished thing. That's the real difference.

artifactLines: Writing voice: Claude's default is less AI-flavored.
Long documents: Claude reads 200 pages without losing track.
File work: Claude desktop app sees your local folder. ChatGPT can't yet.
Multi-step jobs: Claude Cowork runs 30-minute tasks, hands you a finished file.
Images: ChatGPT is currently better at image generation.

beats.narration_text: How to actually start. Go to claude.ai. Create an account — you need to verify your phone. The free plan gives you Sonnet 4.6. Pay twenty dollars a month for Pro and you get Opus 4.7 — the smartest model. Set thinking to High, turn on Thinking mode. That's the setup. Now the infamous prompt. You don't need to write like an engineer. One technique outperforms every prompt template you've ever saved.

shot.slotNote: SLOT — fill media/B03.png (screenshot of Claude model selector showing Opus 4.7 with Thinking set to High — the current interface)

beats.narration_text: The technique is AskUserQuestion. Instead of you writing a perfect prompt, you give Claude a task and tell it to ask you the questions it needs. Claude prompts you. You click answers. The output after three to five questions is better than what you'd get from twenty minutes of prompt engineering. Write it on your hand. 'Help me do X. Use AskUserQuestion first.' That one line puts you ahead of most Claude users.

props.title: the-one-technique.md
props.code:
Help me do [X] for [Y]. Use AskUserQuestion first. # What happens:
# Claude asks you 3-5 questions.
# You click answers or type in "Something else".
# The more you say, the better the final output. # Advanced:
"Give me 3 different strategies."
# Claude shows 3 options to choose from. # Write this on your hand:
# "ask me questions first"

beats.narration_text: Claude has three modes and they do very different things. Chat is the browser or app — type a question, get an answer, copy the text. Cowork is the desktop app's middle tab — it runs multiple Claudes in parallel, generates actual files saved to your computer, and handles 30-minute tasks. Code is for developers building software. If you don't code, you want Chat and Cowork. Cowork is the one that changes how you work.

artifactLines: Chat → browser or app. Type, get answer, copy text.
Cowork → desktop app. Multiple Claudes, real files saved to your computer.
Code → for developers building software.
If you don't code: Chat + Cowork. That's your setup.
Cowork is the tab between Chat and Code. Download the app first.

beats.narration_text: First Cowork session. Download the desktop app at claude.ai/download. Open it, click the Cowork tab. Create one clean folder on your computer — call it Cowork. No passwords, nothing you'd hate to lose — Claude can see everything inside it. Select that folder in Cowork. Now drop in a messy document, an old proposal, last month's numbers. Use the AskUserQuestion trick. Three minutes later: a clean Excel file, a real PDF, sitting in your folder.

props.artifactHeading: Five steps to your first real output
artifactLines: 1. Download the app: claude.ai/download
2. Open app → click Cowork tab.
3. Create one clean folder on your computer. Call it Cowork.
4. Select it inside Cowork → Claude sees everything inside.
5. Drop in a file. Use AskUserQuestion. Watch it build.
Output lands in your folder as a real file.

beats.narration_text: How the people who use Claude well actually use it. Skills and Connectors. A Skill is a slash command — a capability you call from any chat. Type /skill-name and Claude runs your pre-written instructions without you repeating them. A Connector bridges Claude to an app you already pay for: Gmail, Slack, Notion, Granola, HubSpot. Ask Claude 'what's pending in my Gmail this week?' and it reads your actual inbox. Zero copy-paste. That's the real lever.

artifactLines: Skill = a capability called with a slash command.
/linkedin-post → writes a post in your voice, every time.
Connector = Claude reads a live app you already pay for.
Gmail on → 'what's pending this week?' → reads your inbox.
Granola on → 'summarize last week's meetings' → done.
Skills repeat your best work. Connectors bring live context.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Ten minutes this week. Open Claude, pick Opus 4.7, turn on Thinking. Paste this prompt. Don't optimize. Don't craft. Just type your actual task and let Claude ask you the questions.

props.command: Help me do [paste your actual task here]. Use AskUserQuestion first — ask me everything you need before starting. Give me 3 different options when you're done.
